# Mudit Sharma (Learning Design)

Hello everyone. I'm Mo Shema, a teaching fellow and research assistant at the Howard University Graduate School of Education, as well as a learning designer, my passion lies in creating interactive, engaging learning experiences for graduate students and professionals. Over the years, I have seen one challenge repeat itself in the classrooms. Some students dominate discussions while others stay quiet, even when they have some valuable perspectives to share. As a learning designer, I want every learner to feel confident and prepared to contribute. That's not always easy, especially when we are discussing complex, often controversial topics like artificial intelligence in education, climate change, or ethical decision making. This is where chat GPT has been a game changer for me. I have built a custom GPT that acts as a panel of virtual students, each with a unique personality, background and opinion. It's like having a rehearsal for class discussions before they happen so I can prepare for different viewpoints, anticipate challenges, and make my teaching more fun and inclusive. In the next few minutes, I'll take you behind the scenes to show you how I build this custom GPT my thought process and how it's made a measurable difference in student engagement and learning outcomes. Before a class or seminar, I run a discussion simulation on my topic for the day. For example, should AI be used to grade essays? The student respond with realistic, sometimes unexpected arguments. It helped me to design richer, more inclusive conversations and giving every student a voice. So if you are an educator, start small.
